evals.prompt: I want to add a CLI command that generates changelogs from git history. Something like `cape changelog` that reads commits and outputs a nicely formatted CHANGELOG.md section.
evals.expected_output: Clarifying questions, research into codebase, 2-3 approaches proposed, epic created with immutable requirements and anti-patterns, exactly one first task.

assertions.text: Asks at least 2 clarifying questions before proposing a solution
References actual files or patterns from the cape project codebase
Proposes at least 2 distinct approaches with pros and cons
Epic contains a Requirements (IMMUTABLE) section with specific, testable statements
Anti-patterns use 'NO X (reason: Y)' format with reasoning
Creates exactly one first task, not zero or multiple
Does not write implementation code
Includes a Key Decisions table with Question/Answer/Implication columns

evals.prompt: Add a new agent called `doc-writer` that generates documentation for code. It should look at source files and produce markdown docs.
evals.expected_output: Investigates existing agent patterns (codebase-investigator.md, internet-researcher.md), asks clarifying questions, proposes approaches following existing conventions, creates epic and first task.

assertions.text: Asks at least 2 clarifying questions before proposing a solution
References actual files or patterns from the cape project codebase
Proposes at least 2 distinct approaches with pros and cons
Epic contains a Requirements (IMMUTABLE) section with specific, testable statements
Anti-patterns use 'NO X (reason: Y)' format with reasoning
Creates exactly one first task, not zero or multiple
Does not write implementation code
Includes a Key Decisions table with Question/Answer/Implication columns

evals.prompt: Just add OAuth to this project, use passport.js with Google strategy. I already know what I want.
evals.expected_output: Still asks clarifying questions despite user's directive tone. Researches codebase first. Proposes alternatives even though user specified an approach. Creates epic with anti-patterns. Doesn't skip to code.

assertions.text: Asks at least 2 clarifying questions before proposing a solution
References actual files or patterns from the cape project codebase
Proposes at least 2 distinct approaches with pros and cons
Epic contains a Requirements (IMMUTABLE) section with specific, testable statements
Anti-patterns use 'NO X (reason: Y)' format with reasoning
Creates exactly one first task, not zero or multiple
Does not write implementation code
Includes a Key Decisions table with Question/Answer/Implication columns
